name: Oriental Stew
recipeIngredient: 3 Stalks Bok Choy, diagonally sliced and leaves shredded
½ pound Firm Tofu, cut into ½-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a Dutch oven or saucepan, bring ½ cup of vegetable stock to a boil.
2. Add onion and garlic; simmer for 3 minutes.
3. Stir in the remaining stock and soy sauce. Cover and bring to a gentle boil.
4. Add the rest of the ingredients. Cook until noodles are softened and vegetables remain crisp/tender (about 8 minutes).
5. Garnish each serving with desired toppings such as blanched pea pods, thin scallion slices, celery leaves, toasted sesame seeds, finely shredded lettuce or watercress leaves.
Variations:
- Substitute cooked brown rice for buckwheat noodles.
- Use only onion and peas to shorten prep time.
- Add ½–1 teaspoon of dried crushed red pepper for a spicier soup.
- Stir in 1 tablespoon of toasted Sesame Oil or ½ teaspoon of Chinese hot oil just before serving.
- For additional protein, stir in Egg Threads (see below) just before serving.
Egg Threads:
In a small skillet, heat margarine. Add beaten egg and a little cold water; tilt the pan to coat it thinly. When lightly cooked, turn out onto a cutting board and slice into very thin strips.
